title: NOC 73209 – Other repairers and servicers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73209 is a versatile "catch-all" category for highly skilled technical experts who maintain and repair a vast array of specialized products. As a TEER 3 occupation, this group includes specialists for everything from precision musical instruments and professional cameras to industrial scales and vending machines. In 2026, as Canada emphasizes "Right to Repair" legislation and sustainable product lifecycles, these technicians are classified as Circular Economy Essential Talent. For immigration, this role is a hidden gem for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and the 2026 OINP Skilled Trades draws.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level3Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group732 – Building maintenance installers, servicers and repairersMinor Group7320 – Building maintenance installers, servicers and repairersNOC Code73209

duties: Diagnostics: Inspect products, including cameras, musical instruments, and vending machines, to determine the exact cause of malfunction or requirement for repair.
Part Replacement: Repair or replace defective, worn-out, or broken parts and components using hand tools, power tools, or specially designed technical equipment.
Functional Testing: Test and adjust repaired products to ensure they meet manufacturer specifications and proper functioning standards.
Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled maintenance tasks, such as cleaning, lubricating, and replacing filters or seals to prevent future failures.
Calibration: Use precision hand tools and electronic testing equipment to calibrate scales, meters, and sensitive optical instruments.
Blueprint Reading: Read and interpret technical manuals, schematic diagrams, and architectural drawings for complex equipment repair.
Electronic Troubleshooting: Service, repair, and test machines and devices that operate on complex electrical or electronic principles.
Client Consultation: Explain technical issues to customers and provide formal cost estimates for labor and specialized parts.
Documentation: Maintain accurate digital logs of repairs performed, parts used, and time spent on each service call.
Quality Control: Assess the final quality and safety of products before they are returned to the customer or re-entered into service.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Training: Completion of college or other courses relevant to the specific product (e.g., bicycle mechanics or camera repair) is usually necessary.
Experience: Completion of several months of on-the-job training is often required to master specialized tools and techniques.
Certifications:A valid driver’s license is required for field service roles (vending, meters).Specific industry certifications (e.g., CTT for vending machine techs) are highly valued in 2026.
A valid driver’s license is required for field service roles (vending, meters).
Specific industry certifications (e.g., CTT for vending machine techs) are highly valued in 2026.
Digital dexterity and manual stability for working with small, precision components are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, specialized Musical Instrument Technicians or Scale Calibrators in high-demand urban centers often earn annual salaries between $56,000 and $78,000.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate to Good. While digital transformation is changing the nature of some products, the physical need for specialized repair remains a human-centric skill. Projections indicate that the shift toward high-end hobbies (biking, photography, music) and the expansion of automated retail (vending) will ensure a steady stream of job openings through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for other repairers and servicers in 2026 is Stable with Niche Shortages. While general manufacturing has cooled, specialty repair shops are seeing a 15% increase in volume as Canadians opt to repair high-value items rather than replace them. In Alberta and Ontario, there is a specific shortage of Vending Machine Technicians and Home Health Care Equipment Repairers. According to 2026 Job Bank data, approximately 54% of these roles are within the Retail Trade sector, while 13% are in Specialized Services, offering a variety of work environments from small workshops to field service.
Technical Diversity: Gain experience with a huge range of mechanical and electronic components, from antique pianos to AI-integrated vending systems.
Entrepreneurial Potential: This NOC has a high rate of self-employment, making it perfect for those who want to eventually open their own specialty repair shop.
Balanced Work-Life: Most roles under this NOC involve standard 35–40 hour work weeks in controlled indoor environments.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 3 role was included in March 2026 OINP Skilled Trades targeting, showing that provincial governments recognize the value of these diverse technical skills.
pathways: Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): A reliable route for repairers with 2 years of experience and a valid job offer or certificate of qualification.
Ontario (OINP) Skilled Trades Stream: Frequently targets NOC 73209 for technicians already working in the province.
Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Included in category-based selection for those with 6 months of experience in a relevant trade role.
Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): Ideal for specialty technicians with a job offer from an approved employer in Saskatchewan.
